mission.title: contour: Add IP allowlist and blocklist functionality
mission.description: Add IP allowlist and blocklist functionality. Requested by 31+ users.

steps.title: Check current contour deployment
steps.description:
Verify your contour version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n projectcontour -l app.kubernetes.io/name=contour
helm list -n projectcontour 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working contour installation.

steps.title: Review contour configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant contour configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n projectcontour -l app.kubernetes.io/name=contour
kubectl get configmap -n projectcontour -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=contour
```
As either a cluster admin or application developer, I want to be able to restrict access to services behind Contour by source IP address.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add IP allowlist and blocklist functionality
steps.description:
_Posted by  on #4450:_

> **Please describe the problem you have** [A clear, concise, description of the problem you are facing. What is the _problem_ that feature X would solve for you?]
> 
> Feature Request for adding feature to create IP whitelist or Deny list for the services accessed through Contour Ingress
> 
> Positive security model by implementing whitelist or negative security model using deny lists for IP Addresses.
> 
> We need IP whitelisting or denylisting based on X-Forwarded-For

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n projectcontour -l app.kubernetes.io/name=contour
kubectl get events -n projectcontour --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Add IP allowlist and blocklist functionality" is functioning correctly.
